name: Nested Navigation
when_to_use: Use when an application has deep, hierarchical structure with many sections and subsections that users move between, such as documentation, settings trees, admin suites, and large multi-area products.
when_not_to_use: Avoid when the structure is shallow (flat or mega-menu navigation is simpler), when deep nesting hides important destinations too many levels down, or when the hierarchy is unstable and users rely on search instead.
information_structure: A tree of sections and subsections, typically a persistent sidebar with expandable groups, reflecting parent-child relationships in the information architecture down two or more levels.
navigation: Expand/collapse groups in a sidebar to reveal children; breadcrumbs show depth and allow upward jumps; the current location is clearly highlighted at every level.
responsive_behaviour: Collapse the sidebar into a drawer or off-canvas menu on narrow viewports; preserve the expanded path to the current page when reopened.
accessibility_notes: Use tree or nested-list semantics with aria-expanded on group toggles
Mark the current page with aria-current so it is announced
Keep the entire tree keyboard-navigable and the collapsed mobile drawer focus-trapped while open
Provide breadcrumbs as a labelled navigation landmark
evidence: Documentation sites and large admin consoles validate nested sidebar navigation for deep IA
WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices define accessible tree view, breadcrumb, and disclosure navigation patterns
